title: Nikon Automated Microcopy: Imaging Regions Across Multiple Coverslips
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6t50g","text":"An imaging protocol to perform images on regions over multiple coverslips (slides, or wells), with automatic escaping between each coverslip (slide or well). User defined regions are manually drawn onto a low magnification overview of each coverslip (slide or well), after which I higher magnification rescan is performed over all regions. The microscope will automatically escape and refocus the objective when moving between each coverslip (slide or well). ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Power on the microscope and launch NIS Elements

Import the JOB from the .bin file '3 Slide Overview to High Mag Well Tags_Comments added' following the linked protocol Nikon Automated Microscopy: Import a JOB .bin file into JOBs Explorer by Mark Rigby
Focus the microscope on the sample using the objective intended for low magnification scanning Note: this protocol was optimised for a 4x magnification overview scan
In the JOBS Explorer click on the green play icon next to the JOB protocol to launch the JOB wizards
In the JOBs GUI firstly choose the channels with which you wish to image the low magnification overview using the capture definition tab.

Next, click the 'Define Plate' Tab. This protocol was set up with a single slide and three coverslips. It is also possible to generate custom well plates to simulate any range of slides, and coverslips. Alternatively, it would be possible to use a standard well plate or dish by selecting this from 'Select from DB'. In all cases align using the 'Re-Align Plate Holder' button if not already aligned.
Using the select wells tab, select the coverslips (or wells if using a plate), that are to be imaged.
Using the select objectives tab, select the objectives for low magnification overview and high magnification rescanning

Define the settings for high magnification rescanning. Please use only the Z and Lambda Tabs. The 'Z' tab allows a z-stack to be included and the 'Lambda' tab defines which channels will be used. A tick in the box next to the name indicates that these settings will be used.
Once every tab has been visited and checked, click 'Run' at the bottom right of the JOBS GUI to start the acquisition.
Now follow the instructions on the JOBs window, manually go live to confirm focus and then click 'OK'

Once the first coverslip has been scanned, a new window will appear of the stitched image. Please use draw regions function of the JOBs window to draw regions for high magnification rescanning onto the document. Note: regions must be drawn onto the newly opened document behind the JOBs window.
Repeat step 12 for each coverslip (or well), following acquisition of the low magnification scans
Once the nosepiece has changed to the high magnification objective, follow the instructions on the JOBs window, manually go live to confirm focus and then click 'OK'.
Wait for the JOB run to finish
Repeat step 14 for each coverslip (or well)
creator: Alex Knowles
